cards.back: A set of production rules that define a relationship between a set of variables and a set of predicates

cards.front: What is a production rule in First-Order Rules?
cards.back: A rule of the form 'if P1 and P2 and ... and Pn, then Q', where P1, P2, ..., Pn are predicate symbols and Q is a predicate symbol

cards.front: What is the purpose of First-Order Rules?
cards.back: To define a set of rules that can be used to make predictions or classifications based on a set of input variables

cards.front: What is a key characteristic of First-Order Rules?
cards.back: They are based on a set of predefined rules that define relationships between variables and predicates

cards.front: What is the advantage of using First-Order Rules?
cards.back: They can be used to make predictions or classifications in a transparent and interpretable way
